etymology.description: 止(止) (zhǐ, 'foot') + 舟(舟) (zhōu, 'boat') = foot on a boat moving forward -> 止 simplified to 䒑 and 舟 to 月 with 刀(刀) (dāo, 'knife') added
etymology.interpretation: Originally, 前 depicted a foot moving a boat forward, symbolizing advance or progress. Later, components were simplified and a knife was added, linking to its historical connection as the original form of 剪(剪) (jiǎn, 'to cut').

examples.chinese: 我站在房子前面(我站在房子前面)。
examples.english: I am standing in front of the house.

examples.chinese: 前几天我去北京旅游(前幾天我去北京旅遊)。
examples.pinyin: Qián jǐ tiān wǒ qù Běijīng lǚyóu.
examples.english: A few days ago, I went to Beijing for a trip.

examples.chinese: 他比我到得更早(他比我到得更早)，已经在咖啡店前等我(已經在咖啡店前等我)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǐ wǒ dào de gèng zǎo, yǐjīng zài kāfēidiàn qián děng wǒ.
examples.english: He arrived earlier than me and was already waiting for me in front of the café.
